items.title: Where integration tests sit in the test pyramid
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB E2E["End-to-End tests
(few, seconds, full system)"] INT["Integration tests
(some, hundreds of ms, multi-component)"] UN["Unit tests
(many, ms, isolated)"] E2E --- INT --- UN style INT fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
items.caption: Integration tests bridge the speed/realism gap between unit and E2E tests.

items.title: What integration tests actually integrate
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Code["Your code"]
  DB[(Real DB)]
  LLM["Real LLM API"]
  Cache[(Real cache)]
  Code -- HTTP --> LLM
  Code -- SQL --> DB
  Code -- key/value --> Cache
  Test["Integration test"] -. spins up real .-> DB
  Test -. records cassette .-> LLM
  Test -. exercises real .-> Code
items.caption: An integration test wires your code together with a real (or replayed-real) dependency to verify the contract holds.
